summary: Well-structured parent scripting workflow with proper preview-before-apply enforcement, rollback support, and parent-mode gating. Polish-first LLM prompts for explain and refactor. Clean port/adapter separation. 1 medium + 4 low findings.

findings.title: LLM explain/refactor responses returned without output moderation
findings.detail: explain_script and suggest_refactor pass raw script code to the LLM and return the response directly via _llm.complete(prompt). The LLM output is not moderated through ModerationPort.check_text(). While this is parent-mode, the dual-filter moderation pattern used elsewhere in the engine (e.g., RequestAICreationHelpService Step 8) should apply to all AI-generated text. An LLM that produces unsafe content in explanations would bypass moderation entirely.
findings.recommendation: Add optional ModerationPort dependency and run check_text on LLM responses before returning. On BLOCK, return a safe alternative or generic explanation.

findings.title: Mutation ID uses non-cryptographic hash with collision risk
findings.detail: _mutation_id uses GDScript .hash() which is not collision-resistant. If the clock has low resolution (same timestamp for consecutive calls), two mutations to the same project/path could get the same mutation_id, overwriting the pending mutation entry.
findings.recommendation: Append an incrementing counter or use a UUID-like construction to ensure uniqueness.

findings.title: _pending_mutations is unbounded and never pruned for abandoned previews
findings.detail: Applied mutations are erased from _pending_mutations (line 136), but abandoned previews (previewed but never applied) persist forever. A parent previewing many potential changes without applying them will accumulate stale entries.
findings.recommendation: Add a max_pending limit or time-based expiry for previewed mutations that are never applied.

findings.title: No moderation of new_code content in preview_mutation
findings.detail: preview_mutation accepts arbitrary new_code without content checks. While this is parent-mode, if a parent applies code containing unsafe content that subsequently becomes part of a published world, the moderation is only caught at publish time (if at all). Adding a WARN-level check at preview time could provide early feedback.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding an optional moderation check on new_code content with WARN severity for parent mode.

findings.title: Line diff algorithm is naive index-based comparison
findings.detail: _build_line_diff compares old and new lines by index position. If a single line is inserted in the middle, all subsequent lines appear as changes. This is acceptable for MVP but produces noisy diffs for insertions/deletions.
findings.recommendation: Document as a known limitation. A future improvement could use longest common subsequence (LCS) for more accurate diffs.

positive_observations: Clean parent-mode gate on ALL operations via _can_access_parent_mode(actor)
Preview-before-apply enforcement — apply only works with previewed mutation IDs
Rollback restores exact previous script source with proper action log recording
Polish-first LLM prompts: 'Wyjasnij ten skrypt prostym jezykiem dla rodzica', 'Zaproponuj bezpieczny refaktor'
Clean ScriptRepositoryPort/InMemoryScriptRepository separation with composite key pattern
Explicit diff output in preview showing removed (-) and added (+) lines
33 test assertions across 3 contracts covering parent gate, kid blocking, full mutation lifecycle
